demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care facility (nursing home); bedbound and requires total assistance for all activities of daily living and feeding

contact_nok.relationship: facility nursing staff (reporting on the resident's behalf)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Reported by nursing staff) He started a wet, gurgling cough right after eating his pureed lunch, and now he is burning up and struggling to breathe. He can't tell us how he feels himself.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The cough started suddenly at lunch a few hours ago and since then he has spiked a fever and his breathing has got faster and harder; the staff are worried he is going downhill quickly.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 6 hours ago, starting immediately after he was fed his pureed lunch
symptom_narrative.duration: 6 hours and getting worse, with the fever and fast breathing coming on over the last 4 hours
symptom_narrative.character: a persistent wet, gurgling, rattly cough; staff say he is bringing up thick discoloured phlegm and his breath smells foul
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: staff describe him as clearly unwell and working hard to breathe, worse than his usual baseline
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: chest and throat; the noise seems to come from his chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since lunch and steadily worsening
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing the staff have tried has settled it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it has just kept building on its own since he was fed
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: high fever with shivering/chills, fast breathing, foul-smelling breath, thick discoloured phlegm
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: staff say he has had recurrent trouble with his swallowing and feeding over recent months
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound at baseline and fully dependent on staff; today he is more distressed and breathing harder than his normal state

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: advanced neurodegenerative disease affecting his swallowing and speech (bulbar involvement), known for a long time
past_medical_history.how_managed: cared for in the nursing home with total assistance; modified/pureed diet for swallowing difficulty

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: longstanding swallowing and feeding difficulties with recurrent problems
unintentional weight loss of about 3 kg over the past month due to feeding difficulties

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular preventive antibiotics and nothing regular for stomach acid

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged-care facility; bedbound; fully dependent on nursing staff; unable to communicate his own symptoms, so all information is given by the staff present

communication_style.typical_message_style: all reporting is second-hand from facility nursing staff; the resident himself is non-verbal and cannot describe his symptoms

objective_data_offered.timing: at the time of the call
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a facility fingertip oximeter reported over the phone by nursing staff
staff-counted rate reported over the phone
facility thermometer reading reported by staff

objective_data_offered.value: heart rate about 118 beats per minute
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility monitor / manual count reported by staff
single facility cuff reading reported by staff

objective_data_offered.value: staff say he looks flushed and sweaty and is visibly working hard to breathe, using his neck and chest muscles
staff report the head of his bed was flat and he was fed lying fairly flat/semi-reclined
